mnemonics.title: Network Types Order by Size
mnemonics.content: PAN < LAN < CAN < MAN < WAN - Remember: Please Let Captain Mary Visit Wales (P-L-C-M-V-W)

mnemonics.title: PAN Technology
mnemonics.content: Panasonic Uses Bluetooth Regularly - P for Personal, U for USB, B for Bluetooth (PB uses Bluetooth)

mnemonics.title: LAN Speed Remember
mnemonics.content: L - Local & Lightning fast; A - Always 100+ Mbps; N - Network at high speed

acronyms.usage: Connecting personal devices like smartphones, wearables via Bluetooth
Connecting devices in a building or campus using Ethernet/Wi-Fi
Connecting multiple LANs across a city using fiber optics
Connecting networks across countries using satellites and routers
Connecting buildings in a university or corporate campus
Connecting smart home devices and IoT gadgets in a residence
Connecting vehicles for communication, safety and entertainment

acronyms.expansion: Institute of Electrical and Electronics Engineers
acronyms.usage: Standards body for networking technologies like Wi-Fi and Ethernet

visualTips.visualization: Imagine concentric circles: PAN (smallest, 10m) at center, surrounded by LAN (100m-2km), then CAN (1-10km), then MAN (10-100km), then WAN (largest, global) - like ripples in water spreading outward.
Draw an inverse triangle - at top (LAN): high speed, small coverage; at bottom (WAN): lower speed, large coverage - shows the trade-off between speed and distance.
Tree structure with WAN as trunk, MAN as main branches, CAN as smaller branches, LAN as leaves, and PAN as individual cells - shows how smaller networks connect to form larger ones.

analogies.analogy: PAN is like a conversation between two people standing next to each other; WAN is like a phone call across continents - same purpose (communication) but different scales.
LAN is like a local neighborhood where everyone shares a community center - resources are close and easily shared.
MAN is like a city's public transportation system connecting different neighborhoods (LANs) within the city.
Think of road networks: PAN is a footpath, LAN is a street, CAN is a campus road, MAN is a highway across a city, WAN is national highways connecting cities.
